title: Crataegus flexuosa
taxonomy: Crataegus flexuosa is classified within the family Rosaceae, specifically within the subfamily Amygdaloideae. This classification places it among other flowering plants that produce showy flowers and edible fruits, such as apples and plums. Within the genus Crataegus, C. flexuosa is one of the approximately 700 species of hawthorn trees and shrubs. The exact classification within the genus is likely to be at the subgenus or series level, but this information is not readily available. Further research would be needed to determine the specific classification within the genus.
uses: Crataegus flexuosa, a lesser-known species of hawthorn, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditional medicine has used various hawthorn species for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular benefits. In some cultures, hawthorn extracts have been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. However, the specific medicinal uses of Crataegus flexuosa are not well-documented. Further research is necessary to determine the safety and effectiveness of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
properties: Crataegus flexuosa, a species of hawthorn, is known for its potential therapeutic properties. Its flowers, leaves, and fruits are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Research suggests that the plant may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular benefits. It is often used to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure, as well as to improve heart health. However, more studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Crataegus flexuosa as a medicinal treatment.
constituents: Crataegus flexuosa contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These compounds may contribute to the plant's potential health benefits. The flavonoids present in Crataegus flexuosa may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the phenolic acids in the plant may have antimicrobial and antidiabetic effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents of Crataegus flexuosa and their potential uses.

intro: Crataegus flexuosa, also known as the flexible hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its thorny branches and showy white flowers. The health benefits of C. flexuosa include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of C. flexuosa include flavonoids, such as quercetin and vitexin, as well as oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) and phenolic acids. The flexible hawthorn is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
